title: Modes, Padding, Malleability, and Legacy Decryption · Guided Systems Studio
summary: A product-security pair incrementally builds mode failure laboratory and migration adapter, changes one attacker-controlled input, and keeps earlier Go, accessibility, secret-lifecycle, testing, and evidence invariants active. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of mode failure laboratory and migration adapter while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Demonstrate deterministic block-pattern leakage and prohibit ECB for application messages.
Trace CBC chaining, IV requirements, padding validation, malleability, and oracle behavior as a legacy risk.
Trace CTR keystream generation and prove that key-counter reuse destroys confidentiality and enables malleation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Ecb Pattern Leak
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 8771 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ECB and unauthenticated modes while containing CBC or CTR to uniform decrypt-only migration; cap bytes, work, memory, time, attempts, concurrency, and retained secret material before use; then repeat or roll back a nonce, sequence, or key version and reject the stale state. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Ecb Pattern Leak.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for demonstrate deterministic block-pattern leakage and prohibit ecb for application messages. For Cryptography and Go Commit to the mission model · Workshop Crypto Go Ecb Pattern Leak, keep Crypto Go Ecb Pattern Leak connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 5865 during a backup restoration drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ECB and unauthenticated modes while containing CBC or CTR to uniform decrypt-only migration; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then rotate the active key while old data remains readable and new writes cannot downgrade. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Build a bounded decrypt-only adapter that authenticates format, suppresses oracles, records migration, and never emits new legacy ciphertext.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Supporting legacy decryption requires keeping legacy encryption enabled.

steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop cryptographic engineering team handles case 2627 during a signed release review. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ECB and unauthenticated modes while containing CBC or CTR to uniform decrypt-only migration; keep browser execution deterministic and move production entropy, native timing, non-allowlisted crypto, network, HSM, and compliance claims to authorized transfer gates; then remove one transfer gate and state exactly which production claim is no longer supported. Browser code uses pure state models or the narrow side-effect-free crypto allowlist with fixed fixtures. Production entropy and keys, secret custody, native timing and side channels, networks, TLS, HSM/KMS, certificate systems, FIPS or compliance state, load, faults, restore, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Build a bounded decrypt-only adapter that authenticates format, suppresses oracles, records migration, and never emits new legacy ciphertext.

One precondition fails while output still looks plausible. What is the correct response?
